segments.text: That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat by the sea.
Such large crowds gathered around Him that He got into a boat and sat down, while all the people stood on the shore.
And He told them many things in parables, saying,
A farmer went out to sow his seed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And as he was sowing, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Some fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly because the soil was shallow.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But when the sun rose, the seedlings were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the seedlings.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Still other seed fell on good soil and produced a crop—a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He who has ears,[^82] let him hear.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then the disciples came to Jesus and asked,
Why do You speak to the people in parables?
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: He replied,
The knowledge of the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven has been given to you, but not to them.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: Whoever has will be given more, and he will have an abundance. Whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text:
This is why I speak to them in parables:
‘Though seeing, they do not see; though hearing, they do not hear or understand.’[^83]
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text:
In them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled:
‘You will be ever hearing but never understanding; you will be ever seeing but never perceiving.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text:
For this people’s heart has grown callous; they hardly hear with their ears,
and they have closed their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears,
understand with their hearts, and turn, and I would heal them.’[^84]
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: But blessed are your eyes because they see, and your ears because they hear.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text:
For truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous men longed to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.

## The Parable of the Sower Explained
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: Consider, then, the parable of the sower:
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: When anyone hears the message of the kingdom but does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in his heart. This is the seed sown[^85] along the path.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: The seed sown on rocky ground is the one who hears the word and at once receives it with joy.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: But since he has no root, he remains for only a season. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, he quickly falls away.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: The seed sown among the thorns is the one who hears the word, but the worries of this life and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: But the seed sown on good soil is the one who hears the word and understands it. He indeed bears fruit and produces a crop—a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: Jesus put before them another parable:
The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: But while everyone was asleep, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and slipped away.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: When the wheat sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: The owner’s servants came to him and said, ‘Sir, didn’t you sow good seed in your field? Where then did the weeds come from?’
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text:
‘An enemy did this,’ he replied.
So the servants asked him, ‘Do you want us to go and pull them up?’
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: ‘No,’ he said, ‘if you pull the weeds now, you might uproot the wheat with them.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: Let both grow together until the harvest. At that time I will tell the harvesters: First collect the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned; then gather the wheat into my barn.’
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

text: The Parable of the Mustard Seed

segments.text: He put before them another parable:
The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man planted in his field.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, yet it grows into the largest of garden plants and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the air come and nest in its branches.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: He told them still another parable:
The kingdom of heaven is like leaven that a woman took and mixed into three measures of flour, until all of it was leavened.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

text: I Will Open My Mouth in Parables

segments.text: Jesus spoke all these things to the crowds in parables. He did not tell them anything without using a parable.
So was fulfilled what was spoken through the prophet:
I will open My mouth in parables; I will utter things hidden since the foundation of the world.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

text: The Parable of the Weeds Explained

segments.text: Then Jesus dismissed the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to Him and said,
Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: He replied,
The One who sows the good seed is the Son of Man.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: The field is the world, and the good seed represents the sons of the kingdom. The weeds are the sons of the evil one,
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: and the enemy who sows them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age, and the harvesters are angels.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: As the weeds are collected and burned in the fire, so will it be at the end of the age.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: The Son of Man will send out His angels, and they will weed out of His kingdom every cause of sin and all who practice lawlessness.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: And they will throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text:
Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father.[^87]
He who has ears, let him hear.

## The Parables of the Treasure and the Pearl
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. When a man found it, he hid it again, and in his joy he went and sold all he had and bought that field.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text:
When he found one very precious pearl, he went away and sold all he had and bought it.

## The Parable of the Net
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: Once again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that was cast into the sea and caught all kinds of fish.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: When it was full, the men pulled it ashore. Then they sat down and sorted the good fish into containers, but threw the bad away.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: So will it be at the end of the age: The angels will come and separate the wicked from the righteous,
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: and throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: Have you understood all these things?
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: Yes,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they answered.
Then He told them,
For this reason, every scribe who has been discipled in the kingdom of heaven is like a homeowner who brings out of his storeroom new treasures as well as old.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When Jesus had finished these parables, He withdrew from that place.
Coming to His hometown, He taught the people in their synagogue, and they were astonished.
Where did this man get such wisdom and miraculous powers?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked.
Isn’t this the carpenter’s son? Isn’t His mother’s name Mary, and aren’t His brothers James, Joseph,[^88] Simon, and Judas?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Aren’t all His sisters with us as well? Where then did this man get all these things?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
And they took offense at Him.
But Jesus said to them,
Only in his hometown and in his own household is a prophet without honor.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And He did not do many miracles there, because of their unbelief.
